2606.09403.title: Introducing multiplex semantic networks as multifaceted representations of creative associative knowledge across multilingual samples
2606.09403.paper_summary: Creativity is a complex cognitive ability that relies on knowledge organisation and retrieval from semantic memory. Yet most research uses a single task to measure it, capturing only a fraction of this complexity. This study investigates multiplex networks - layered semantic networks obtained from six cognitive tasks - as a more comprehensive approach to modelling the associative knowledge underlying creativity. We collected data from N=518 individuals from four countries (Austria, USA, Singapore, Italy). From their responses to verbal fluency, sentence-chain, free association, and narrative writing tasks, we constructed semantic networks and assembled them in a multiplex structure. AI persona-based responses provided a comparison baseline. Structural reducibility analyses showed that different task layers captured distinct, non-redundant information about semantic organisation, supporting the use of multiple tasks over any single one. The networks from high- and low-creative groups remained structurally distinct, while AI-generated networks showed near-identical structures regardless of creativity group. Finally, we used 12 features (network measures, emotional scores, and spreading activation simulations) in a machine learning model using ridge regression to predict individual creativity scores. The combination of structurally similar layers, as identified in the previous stage, improved a proof-of-concept prediction accuracy by 50%. Structural measures showed the highest feature importance, with spreading activation dynamics providing additional predictive power. Together, these findings indicate that multiplex semantic networks capture a richer, cross-cultural picture of associative knowledge underlying creativity. We also release our diverse dataset and code to foster diverse computational approaches within the creativity community.
2606.09403.paper_summary_zh: 創造力是一種複雜的認知能力，依賴於知識的組織和從語義記憶中檢索。<lb/>然而，大多數研究使用單一任務來測量它，只捕捉到這種複雜性的一小部分。<lb/>本研究探討多重網絡——從六個認知任務中獲得的分層語義網絡——作為建模創造力背後聯想知識的更全面的方法。<lb/>我們從四個國家（奧地利、美國、新加坡、意大利）收集了N=518個個體的數據。<lb/>根據他們在口語流暢性、句子鏈、自由聯想和敘事寫作任務中的反應，我們構建了語義網絡並將其組裝成多重結構。<lb/>基於AI角色的反應提供了一個比較基準。<lb/>結構可約性分析顯示，不同的任務層捕捉到關於語義組織的不同且不冗餘的信息，支持使用多個任務而不是單一任務。<lb/>高創造力和低創造力群體的網絡在結構上保持明顯區別，而AI生成的網絡無論創造力群體如何都顯示出幾乎相同的結構。<lb/>最後，我們在一個使用脊迴歸的機器學習模型中使用了12個特徵（網絡度量、情感分數和擴散激活模擬）來預測個體創造力分數。<lb/>在前一階段識別出的結構相似層的組合使概念驗證的預測準確性提高了50%。<lb/>結構度量顯示出最高的特徵重要性，擴散激活動態提供了額外的預測能力。<lb/>總體而言，這些發現表明，多重語義網絡捕捉到了創造力背後聯想知識的更豐富的跨文化圖景。<lb/>我們還發布了我們多樣的數據集和代碼，以促進創造力社區內部的多樣計算方法。
2606.09403.author: Edith Haim et.al.
2606.09403.authors: Edith Haim, Kurt Haim, Roger E. Beaty, Cynthia S. Q. Siew, Massimo Stella
